content:
===center
![tyranid](https://preview.redd.it/cns8okd8ksk61.jpg?width=1080&crop=smart&auto=webp&s=428db7400f6ce85839dfba9240065585aceb4d8a)

*It is a galaxy-conquering hivemind organism.*

*Built for combat, bred for war.*

*An otherworldy apex predator.*

===endcenter

===accordion
===panel:Introduction

## Introduction

In Warhammer lore, the Tyranids are a vicious, galaxy-conquering swarm of massive reptilian/insectoid creatures whose hunger is limited only by the amount of consumable prey in said galaxy.  They are resilient, ever-evolving and ever-growing apex predators at the top of the galactic food chain, committed to ravaging every last resource on each world they conquer.

This deck has been built to embrace that philosophy and the Tyranids' endless hunger.  It generates massive amounts of mana and sinks it into absolutely gargantuan X spells and creatures who never stop growing with +1/+1 counters.  There is no end to Tyranid evolution, there is no such thing as "big enough", there is only hunger.

Who do we have commanding this ravenous hivemind?  [[Magus Lucea Kane]], whose ability not only lets us evolve our Tyranid army, but also duplicate them to apply immense pressure on any who oppose the swarm.

===endpanel
===panel:Pros, Cons & Power Level

## Pros, Cons & Power Level

✅ **Pros**

- This is very much a flavor-focused deck more than a competitive one.  If you're looking for a Tyranid tribal deck that plays a decent number of tyranids and can at least hang with most casual (non-cEDH) decks, this is it.

- It's fun.  I can't say much more about it, it's just fun to swarm the field with otherworldy alien monsters and double them with [[Magus Lucea Kane]].  This is a Tyranid Timmy deck through and through.

❌ **Cons**

- This particular build is not the most competitive.  There is definitely room for powering up.  More counterspells and less "win more" cards would be the place to start making upgrades.

- While it can function fine without its commander, it's at its strongest when MLK is doubling your X spells.  When opponents realize this, they will try to keep her off the field.

☢️ **Power Level**

- This deck is casual.  It is *borderline* high-power casual.  Only reason I don't give it that title is its lack of combos and reliance on combat damage to win games.

- Just big, dumb creatures turning sideways.  There's not much trickery or cheeky win-cons in here.

===center

![tyranid3](https://i.imgur.com/nzSH5eK.jpg)

*Genetically engineered to be the ultimate living weapon*

===endcenter

===endpanel
===panel:The Gameplan

## The Gameplan

A couple of things to note regarding [[Magus Lucea Kane]]'s ability:

===accordion
===panel:The activation cannot be responded to (by anyone) because it is a mana ability

- Mana abilities do not use the stack and therefore nobody can do anything when you tap MLK for mana.  The rest of the text is also tied to that ability, so it too cannot be responded to.

- We get the [[symbol:c]][[symbol:c]] and then create a **delayed** trigger which occurs only when we put an X-spell on the stack that turn.

- That trigger **CAN** be responded to like any other spell/ability, so an opponent could [[Disallow]] it when it goes on the stack.

===endpanel
===panel:Tapping Lucea Kane multiple times means multiple copies

- Tapping Kane multiple times per turn creates multiple delayed triggers that will copy the next X-spell we cast.

- So for example if we tap her, untap her, then tap her again and follow up with an X-spell, it gets copied not once but twice for a 3-for-1 special!

- This is why [[Formidable Speaker]], [[Kiora, Behemoth Beckoner]] and [[Kiora's Follower]] are so strong in this deck.

===endpanel
===panel:A copied X-spell also receives the X value

- In case it wasn't super obvious, Lucea Kane's ability to copy the next X spell you cast will also copy its X value.

- For example, if you cast a [[Mawloc]] for [[symbol:5]][[symbol:r]][[symbol:g]], then the copy will also get X=5, which means you benefit from both of the *Ravenous* triggers.

===endpanel
===endaccordion

The gameplan here is simple.  This is a stompy deck hyper-focused on mana generation and big, REALLY big creatures.

Ramp hard and ramp fast, but refrain from putting [[Magus Lucea Kane]] on the board unless you have an X spell you can cast using the mana she generates.  Otherwise, she's not providing much value and you risk having her be removed by an opponent's spell.  Obviously, if you just need her mana to cast other spells then by all means get her on board, but if you have the luxury of waiting for an impactful X spell, consider saving her for that.

Establish mana dorks and powerful enchantments that care about +1/+1 counters.  Cast powerful Tyranids and other creatures which create or distribute +1/+1 counters.  Use these creatures to begin overwhelming your opponents.

Finally, look to establish a decisive win condition such as [[Herald of Secret Streams]], [[Winged Hive Tyrant]] or [[Primordial Hydra]] to seriously put the hurt on opponents.  This is a stompy deck.  Do some stomping!

While all of this is happening, we do have some tools to protect MLK such as [[Stubborn Denial]], [[Swan Song]], [[An Offer You Can't Refuse]], [[Fierce Guardianship]], [[Deflecting Swat]] and [[Tyrant Guard]].  With the insane draw power this deck has, we shouldn't have too much of an issue having at least some form of protection at all times.  If MLK does get removed, however, we have plenty of ramp to re-cast her later in the game.  One such example is [[Open the Way]].  In a 4-player game, we can cast it for X=4, copied which means a guaranteed 8 lands out of our deck and onto the field.  If you see this card, copy it with Kane and you'll be set for the rest of the game!

===center

![tyranid2](https://www.warhammerart.com/cdn/shop/files/Tyranid-Cover-Art.jpg?v=1741514999&width=1200)

*There is a theory that they have already conquered every other system, that's why we've never received any signals from beyond the cosmic horizon.*

*And that thought is terrifying.*

===endcenter

===endpanel
===panel:Packages

## Packages

===accordion
===panel:Ramp (25)

- [[Animist's Awakening]], [[Open the Way]], [[Farseek]], [[Nature's Lore]], [[Rampant Growth]] and [[Three Visits]] all get lands out of our deck and onto the field.

- [[Genesis Hydra]] can do that as well if we don't find any other permanents we like with its ETB ability.

- [[Sol Ring]], [[Arcane Signet]], [[The Great Henge]], [[Magus Lucea Kane]], [[Birds of Paradise]], [[Elvish Mystic]], [[Fyndhorn Elves]], [[Llanowar Elves]], [[Biophagus]], [[Incubation Druid]] and [[Selvala, Heart of the Wilds]] all generate mana on their own.

- [[Goblin Anarchomancer]] reduces the cost of several of our spells.

- [[Kiora, Behemoth Beckoner]] can untap another permanent which can be used on a mana dork, land or rock to get more mana out of it.

- [[Kiora's Follower]] and [[Formidable Speaker]] can untap another creature which would normally just go even with a lot of the usual dorks but [[Magus Lucea Kane]] creates 2 man so this interaction will not only net us mana but lets her copy the next X spell not once but twice!  And even if we have no creatures to untap, [[Kiora's Follower]] can simply untap one of our lands so she functionally acts as a mana dork on her own.

- [[Goldvein Hydra]] and [[Hell to Pay]] create treasure tokens we can exchange for mana.

- [[Springleaf Parade]] can create a swarm of changeling tokens and then turns into a [[Cryptolith Rite]] for our tokens, allowing them all to tap for mana.

===endpanel
===panel:Removal & Interaction (17)

- [[Rampaging Yao Guai]], [[Aberrant]] and [[Steelbane Hydra]] can get rid of artifacts and/or enchantments.

- [[Mawloc]] and [[Voracious Hydra]] can fight a creature when they ETB, serving as removal if their power is high enough to kill that creature.

- [[Zoanthrope]], [[Shatterskull Smashing]], [[Hell to Pay]] and [[Exocrine]] can deal damage to creatures.

- [[Beast Within]] and [[Cyclonic Rift]] can get rid of any permanent.

- [[Stubborn Denial]], [[An Offer You Can't Refuse]], [[Swan Song]], [[Fierce Guardianship]] and [[Deflecting Swat]] all let us interact with spells on the stack.

- [[Inspiring Call]] can be cast in response to removal to save our board.

===endpanel
===panel:Card Draw & Card Advantage (18)

- [[Hormagaunt Horde]], [[Termagant Swarm]], [[Aberrant]], [[Mawloc]], [[Tervigon]], [[Exocrine]], [[Tyrant Guard]] and [[Broodlord]] all have the Ravenous ability, which lets us draw a card if we sink enough mana into their casting cost.

- [[Hydroid Krasis]] doesn't have *Ravenous* like the Tyranids but it does have its own card draw effect depending on how much mana we sink into its X cost.

- [[Ingenious Prodigy]] can exchange its counters for cards.

- [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]] draws us cards when he enters and repeatedly whenever an opponent searches their library.

- [[Kiora, Behemoth Beckoner]], [[Selvala, Heart of the Wilds]], [[Garruk's Uprising]] and [[The Great Henge]] all draw us cards for playing creatures.

- [[Bred for the Hunt]] draws us cards for attacking with our +1/+1 counter creatures.

- [[Court of Garenbrig]] makes us the Monarch when it ETBs and being the Monarch lets us draw a card on the end of our turn.

- [[Inspiring Call]] is a one-time draw spell.

===endpanel
===panel:Counter Swarm (29)

This section points out cards that create or care about +1/+1 counters, which is one of the main focuses of this deck.

- [[Biophagus]], [[Kami of Whispered Hopes]], [[Benevolent Hydra]] and [[Vorinclex, Monstrous Raider]] all increase the amount of counters our creatures would normally get.

- [[Sphere Grid]], [[Magus Lucea Kane]], [[Court of Garenbrig]], [[The Great Henge]], [[Duskshell Crawler]], [[Biophagus]] and [[Broodlord]] all generate counters and put them on creatures.

- [[Stonecoil Serpent]], [[Ingenious Prodigy]], [[Hormagaunt Horde]], [[Wan Shi Tong, Librarian]], [[Termagant Swarm]], [[Aberrant]], [[Mawloc]], [[Tervigon]], [[Exocrine]], [[Tyrant Guard]], [[Broodlord]], [[Shivan Devastator]], [[Hydroid Krasis]], [[Primordial Hydra]], [[Goldvein Hydra]], [[Steelbane Hydra]], [[Voracious Hydra]] and [[Genesis Hydra]] all come with their own counters.

- [[Duskshell Crawler]], [[Herald of Secret Streams]] and [[Winged Hive Tyrant]] grant our creatures with +1/+1 counters a keyword or stat buff to give them an edge in combat or evade it entirely.

- [[Tyrant Guard]] can protect our creatures with +1/+1 counters.

===endpanel
===panel:Win Conditions

- [[Primordial Hydra]] can get out of control very quickly.  Make it big enough and it can knock out opponents in just a few swings.

- [[Duskshell Crawler]], [[Herald of Secret Streams]] and [[Winged Hive Tyrant]] help us push damage through chump blockers or evade them entirely.

- [[Court of Garenbrig]] and [[Vorinclex, Monstrous Raider]] can push our creatures' power to absurd levels and opponents won't last much longer with these guys on the field.

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===center

![conq](https://i.imgur.com/kV4ZPIX.jpeg)

*To them, we are merely prey.  We always have been.*

===endcenter

===endpanel
===panel:Budget Options

## Budget Options

**The biggest offenders:**

- [[Cyclonic Rift]] can be replaced with [[Curse of the Swine]] or [[Wave Goodbye]].

- [[Fierce Guardianship]] can be replaced with [[Negate]].

- [[Deflecting Swat]] can be replaced with [[Tyvar's Stand]].

- [[Swan Song]] can be replaced with [[Miscast]].

- [[The Great Henge]] can be replaced with [[Rosheen Meanderer]] or [[Elementalist's Palette]].

- [[Vorinclex, Monstrous Raider]] can be replaced with [[Branching Evolution]] or [[Ozolith, the Shattered Spire]].

**The next biggest offenders:**

- Unfortunately, a Temur landbase does not come cheap and this list is playing all of the "good" dual lands which are pretty pricey but you can always substitute budget dual lands instead.  Take your pick from [Gruul Lands](https://managathering.com/duallands/Gruul.html), [Simic Lands](https://managathering.com/duallands/Simic.html) and [Izzet Lands](https://managathering.com/duallands/Izzet.html).

- Keep in mind that this deck was built with Green as the primary color and Blue/Red are secondary.  The land base reflects this, so when you make your substitutions above, make sure the ratios stay the same or as close to the same as possible.  You want to have access to green from nearly every land source so you can cast a majority of your spells.

**The less offensive offenders:**

- I'm playing the fancy full art basic lands from Unfinity: [[Forest|UNF:244]], [[Mountain|UNF:243]] and [[Island|UNF:241]].  These are contributing to the total cost of the deck you see on the Moxfield grand total calculator so if you switch to cheaper basic lands that'll save about $15 from the price of the deck.  I just thought they looked cool and have an outer space theme to go along with the Tyranid swarm.

===endpanel
===panel:Upgrades

## Upgrades

If you want to play this deck at high power, you're going to need A LOT of protection for [[Magus Lucea Kane]].  She's the heart and soul of the deck and the main reason it gets out of control so fast.

I intentionally went for big dumb tyranids because I wanted this deck to be fun and flavorful but if you want to survive at high-power tables, you'll need even more protection like [[Heroic Intervention]], [[Syncopate]], [[Counterspell]], [[Mana Drain]], [[Negate]] and [[Miscast]].

You may also want to include some untappers, since she'll get the most mileage out of her ability the more times she can use it in one turn.  [[Thousand-Year Elixir]] comes to mind.

To make room, start by cutting any of the "win more" or cards like [[Vorinclex, Monstrous Raider]].  Make our strong creatures stronger?  Fun at a low-power table but dead weight at a high-power table.

===endpanel
===panel:Change Log

## Change Log

===accordion
===panel:3/31/24

- [[Kalonian Hydra]] was removed in favor of [[Railway Brawler]] because he doesn't have to attack and doubles our creatures' stats when they enter.

- [[Apocalypse Hydra]] was removed because I was seriously scraping the bottom of the barrel on that one but luckily we got a way better X-creature in the form of [[Goldvein Hydra]]!

- [[Pongify]] and [[Rapid Hybridization]] didn't feel like they were needed.  I wasn't removing creatures that often, rather just running over them with monstrous Tyranids instead.  So in their place I added [[Hell to Pay]] and [[Lightning Greaves]] for some Haste and protection.

===endpanel
===panel:3/12/24

- [[Nature's Claim]] was removed in favor of [[Rampaging Yao Guai]].

===endpanel
===panel:3/7/24

- Finally remembered to pick up a [[Kiora's Follower]] from the LGS.  It took the place of [[Patriar's Seal]] because it helps get MLK into play on turn 3 whereas the Seal does not.

===endpanel
===panel:11/16/23

- Minor swap: [[Thousand-Year Elixir]] was replaced with [[Patriar's Seal]].  I was only really using the Elixir on MLK so the restriction of Legendary only on [[Patriar's Seal]] isn't a huge deal.  Plus it makes mana on its own which gave it the edge over the Elixir in my book.

===endpanel
===panel:9/17/23

- [[Branching Evolution]] felt like a "win more" card and I preferred the utility of [[Thousand-Year Elixir]] instead.  MLK can make [[symbol:c]][[symbol:c]] and create a delayed trigger to copy the next X spell we cast.  We can then spend [[symbol:c]] to untap her and use her effect again, making yet another delayed trigger to copy the next X spell.  We've also gotten another [[symbol:c]][[symbol:c]].  So we've netted 1 mana and the next X spell we cast gets copied twice.  That's insanely good value.

- And [[Inspiring Call]], while a fun way to protect our board, just felt like a more expensive [[Stubborn Denial]] in most situations.  I want to be sinking as much mana as possible into my X spells, so holding up 3 for [[Inspiring Call]] doesn't feel great.  Holding up 1 for [[Stubborn Denial]] feels much better.

- Also wanted to increase the number of X-spells so [[Fractal Harness]] was brought in and [[Tyranid Prime]] was removed.

===endpanel
===panel:9/9/23

- I wasn't finding [[Trygon Prime]] super useful so I decided to replace it with [[Genesis Hydra]].  While it's not particularly good for cheating X-permanents into play, it can still grab some cool stuff and worst-case scenario we just get a land.  The rest of the cards don't get dumped but shuffled back so we don't lose anything either.

- I value utility over "win more" effects, so [[Ozolith, the Shattered Spire]] was replaced with [[Agatha's Soul Cauldron]].  It's been phenomenal in my Ezuri deck.

- And finally, [[Apocalypse Hydra]] provides some targeted removal over [[Savageborn Hydra]] so I made that replacement as well.

===endpanel
===panel:9/6/23

- [megablake](https://www.moxfield.com/users/megablake) suggested [[Incubation Druid]] over [[Bloom Tender]] and I have to agree; I think it's a better mana dork overall in this deck.

- [[Doubling Season]] is good but I had to cut it for a few reasons.  5 mana is a lot for a card that doesn't do much on its own.  It also does not make double tokens with [[Magus Lucea Kane]] because she makes a copy of the spell, she does NOT create a token.  This was the easiest cut I could find for the new [[Court of Garenbrig]].

- [[Tribute to the World Tree]] was removed in favor of [[Ingenious Prodigy]].  Triple green is tough to cast early game and I'd much rather see [[Ingenious Prodigy]] instead so I can draw cards.  Plus, with [[Magus Lucea Kane]], she can keep giving it a counter which we can then remove for an extra card on our upkeep, effectively serving as a [[Phyrexian Arena]].

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===center

![tyranid4](https://cdnb.artstation.com/p/assets/images/images/053/910/247/large/billy-christian-423153-zoanthrope-final-post.jpg?1663302075)

*Some are brutes, some are clever.  But make no mistake - they are ALL deadly.*

===endcenter
